title: cartrema marginata
intro: Cartrema marginata, also known as the Spurge Olive or Wild Olive, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The Spurge Olive has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat various skin conditions and wounds due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an anti-diarrheal agent. Cartrema marginata is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to treat various ailments, and its leaves and fruits are also used in traditional medicine in some African countries.
